title: PieNoPie
learning_objective: Learn to leak stack canary from uninitialized memory and combine with buffer overflow when PIE is disabled.
build_command: docker build --label ctf-factory.managed=true --label ctf-factory.workspace_id=69bdbdf9-6c55-485c-8318-8f7a8b02d124 --label ctf-factory.challenge_id=pwn-09c5542e-0049 --label ctf-factory.category=pwn -t pwn-69bdbd-pienopie:latest -f deploy/Dockerfile .
hints: The binary has no PIE but does have a canary — you know where everything is, but you need to know WHAT to write.
The report viewer prints buffer content before checking bounds — what happens if the buffer wasn't properly initialized?
Find the offset to canary and return address, then construct your payload: [padding][canary][more padding][win address]. The win function is named maintenance_mode in the binary.
validation_notes: Reference solve uses pwntools: (1) trigger uninitialized print to leak canary at offset 136-143, (2) construct payload with 136 bytes padding + canary + 8 bytes RBP + win address, (3) overflow and receive flag.
unintended_solutions: Direct overflow without canary leak — blocked by stack canary
Format string leak — blocked by no format string vulnerability
Brute-force canary — blocked by canary changes per connection
